Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.ui

Class FlixelBar

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelSprite
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.ui.FlixelBar
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDebugDrawable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
public class FlixelBar extends FlixelSprite
A UI bar for progress, health, stamina, experience, cooldowns, loading, or any value mapped to a numeric range. It extends FlixelSprite so you can add instances to a FlixelSpriteGroup, use sprite transforms (position, scale, rotation, tint, alpha) with the rest of your HUD, and rely on the same camera and lifecycle rules as other sprites.

Rendering: The bar draws custom geometry with a shared white-pixel texture and optional TextureRegion backgrounds and fills. It does not use FlixelSprite.loadGraphic(com.badlogic.gdx.files.FileHandle); those entry points are blocked so a bar never accidentally shows a loaded texture on top of the bar UI.

Value and range: You set a logical range with setRange(float, float) and either setValue(float) or setTrack(FloatSupplier) so the fill updates each frame. Use setMaxSupplier(FloatSupplier) when the maximum changes at runtime (for example leveling systems) without calling setRange manually.

Fill direction: FlixelBar.BarFillDirection controls whether the fill grows left-to-right, right-to-left, top-to-bottom, or bottom-to-top.

Smoothing: setLerp(float) applies frame-rate independent smoothing to the displayed value so the bar can lag slightly behind the target, similar to camera follow smoothing in FlixelCamera.

Appearance: Solid colors, custom empty and filled regions, optional two-color gradients, optional border, and threshold-based fill colors with optional color smoothing when the fill percent drops. Optional overlay text uses setText(java.util.function.Consumer) with a FlixelString scratch buffer (see that method for why).

Screen space: With setScreenSpace(boolean) true, the bar is offset by the current draw camera scroll so it stays fixed on screen while the world moves.

    Sets the tint for the empty (background) strip when no setEmptyGraphic(TextureRegion) is set.

    Same as setEmptyColor(Color) using a FlixelColor object.

    setEmptyGraphic(@Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.TextureRegion region)
    Uses a texture region for the full empty background stretched to the bar size.
    FlixelBar

    Sets which edge of the bar is the fill origin and which axis the fill grows along.

    setFilledColor(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color c)
    Sets the tint for the filled portion when no setFilledGraphic(TextureRegion) or gradient is used, unless threshold coloring overrides the fill color.
    FlixelBar
    setFilledColor(@NotNull FlixelColor c)
    Same as setFilledColor(Color) using FlixelColor.
    FlixelBar
    setFilledGraphic(@Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.TextureRegion region)
    Uses a texture region for the fill; the bar crops UVs so only a fraction matching the current percent is shown.
    FlixelBar
    setGradient(@Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color start, @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color end)
    Enables a two-color linear gradient for the filled portion along the fill axis.
    FlixelBar
    setGradient(@Nullable FlixelColor start, @Nullable FlixelColor end)
    Same as setGradient(Color, Color) using FlixelColor.
    FlixelBar
    setLerp(float lerp)
    Smoothing factor for the value animation: 1 means the displayed fill matches the target immediately; values between 0 and 1 apply exponential smoothing scaled by elapsed time and target framerate (same idea as camera follow lerp in FlixelCamera).
    FlixelBar
    setMaxSupplier(@Nullable FloatSupplier maxSupplier)
    Supplies a new maximum each frame (for example current max HP from a stats object).
    FlixelBar
    setRange(float min, float max)
    Sets the inclusive logical range [min, max] used to map getValue() to fill percent.
    FlixelBar
    setScreenSpace(boolean screenSpace)
    When true, each draw adds the current Flixel.getDrawCamera() scroll to the bar position so the bar stays fixed on the monitor while the camera moves.

    When non-null, update(float) sets the target value from FloatSupplier.getAsFloat() each frame (for example player::getHealth).
    FlixelBar

    Sets the target value when not using setTrack(FloatSupplier).
    void
    update(float elapsed)
    Updates sprite animation state, then applies max supplier, value tracking, value smoothing, and refreshes overlay text from the setText(java.util.function.Consumer) formatter when set.

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelBar

      public FlixelBar(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Creates a bar at the given world position with the given hitbox size. No texture is loaded on the sprite; all visuals come from the bar configuration API.
      Parameters:
      x - Left edge in world space (or screen-anchored space if setScreenSpace(boolean) is used).
      y - Top edge (Flixel convention: Y down).
      width - Bar width in pixels; used as the drawable width and for gradient resolution hints.
      height - Bar height in pixels.

  • Method Details

    • loadGraphic

      public final FlixelSprite loadGraphic(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture texture, int frameWidth, int frameHeight)
      Description copied from class: FlixelSprite
      Loads a texture and automatically resizes the size of this sprite.
      Overrides:
      loadGraphic in class FlixelSprite
      Parameters:
      texture - The texture to load onto this sprite (owned by caller).
      frameWidth - How wide the sprite should be.
      frameHeight - How tall the sprite should be.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - always.

    • makeGraphic

      public final FlixelSprite makeGraphic(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Description copied from class: FlixelSprite
      Creates a solid color rectangular texture on the fly.
      Overrides:
      makeGraphic in class FlixelSprite
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the graphic.
      height - The height of the graphic.
      color - The color of the graphic.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - always.

    • setScreenSpace

      public FlixelBar setScreenSpace(boolean screenSpace)
      When true, each draw adds the current Flixel.getDrawCamera() scroll to the bar position so the bar stays fixed on the monitor while the camera moves. When false, the bar uses normal sprite coordinates (moves with the world).
      Parameters:
      screenSpace - true to pin to the viewport in screen space; false for world space.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setFillDirection

      public FlixelBar setFillDirection(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelBar.BarFillDirection direction)
      Sets which edge of the bar is the fill origin and which axis the fill grows along. Changing this may rebuild the internal gradient texture if a gradient is enabled.
      Parameters:
      direction - One of FlixelBar.BarFillDirection; must not be null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getFillDirection

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelBar.BarFillDirection getFillDirection()

    • setRange

      public FlixelBar setRange(float min, float max)
      Sets the inclusive logical range [min, max] used to map getValue() to fill percent. If max is less than min, the two are swapped. Current and displayed values are clamped into the new range.
      Parameters:
      min - Lower bound of the value range (for example 0 for health).
      max - Upper bound (for example max HP). Must define a positive span after ordering for a non-zero fill.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getMin

      public float getMin()

    • getMax

      public float getMax()

    • setMaxSupplier

      public FlixelBar setMaxSupplier(@Nullable @Nullable FloatSupplier maxSupplier)
      Supplies a new maximum each frame (for example current max HP from a stats object). When non-null, update(float) calls setRange(float, float) with the current minimum and the supplied max so the bar stays consistent when max changes without manual range updates.
      Parameters:
      maxSupplier - null to use only setRange(float, float); otherwise polled each update.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getMaxSupplier

      @Nullable public @Nullable FloatSupplier getMaxSupplier()

    • setValue

      public FlixelBar setValue(float value)
      Sets the target value when not using setTrack(FloatSupplier). Ignored while a track supplier is set. If getLerp() is 1, the displayed value snaps immediately; otherwise the displayed value catches up in update(float).
      Parameters:
      value - Logical value clamped to the current setRange(float, float).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getValue

      public float getValue()

    • getDisplayedValue

      public float getDisplayedValue()

    • setTrack

      public FlixelBar setTrack(@Nullable @Nullable FloatSupplier supplier)
      When non-null, update(float) sets the target value from FloatSupplier.getAsFloat() each frame (for example player::getHealth). When null, setValue(float) drives the bar. Primitive supplier avoids boxing.
      Parameters:
      supplier - null for manual values; otherwise the polled value source.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getTrack

      @Nullable public @Nullable FloatSupplier getTrack()

    • setLerp

      public FlixelBar setLerp(float lerp)
      Smoothing factor for the value animation: 1 means the displayed fill matches the target immediately; values between 0 and 1 apply exponential smoothing scaled by elapsed time and target framerate (same idea as camera follow lerp in FlixelCamera). This is separate from setThresholdSmoothing(float, boolean) which only affects threshold colors.
      Parameters:
      lerp - Smoothing amount in [0, 1]; clamped if out of range.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • getLerp

      public float getLerp()

    • setEmptyColor

      public FlixelBar setEmptyColor(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color c)
      Sets the tint for the empty (background) strip when no setEmptyGraphic(TextureRegion) is set. Clears any empty graphic region so the bar uses solid color for the background again.
      Parameters:
      c - libGDX color; not null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setEmptyColor

      public FlixelBar setEmptyColor(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelColor c)
      Same as setEmptyColor(Color) using a FlixelColor object.
      Parameters:
      c - Engine color wrapper; not null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setFilledColor

      public FlixelBar setFilledColor(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color c)
      Sets the tint for the filled portion when no setFilledGraphic(TextureRegion) or gradient is used, unless threshold coloring overrides the fill color. Clears any filled graphic region.
      Parameters:
      c - LibGDX color; not null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setFilledColor

      public FlixelBar setFilledColor(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelColor c)
      Same as setFilledColor(Color) using FlixelColor.
      Parameters:
      c - Engine color wrapper; not null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setEmptyGraphic

      public FlixelBar setEmptyGraphic(@Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.TextureRegion region)
      Uses a texture region for the full empty background stretched to the bar size. Set null to fall back to setEmptyColor(Color).
      Parameters:
      region - Empty-bar art, or null for solid setEmptyColor(Color).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setFilledGraphic

      public FlixelBar setFilledGraphic(@Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.TextureRegion region)
      Uses a texture region for the fill; the bar crops UVs so only a fraction matching the current percent is shown. Set null to use setFilledColor(Color) or setGradient(Color, Color).
      Parameters:
      region - Fill art, or null for color or gradient fill.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setGradient

      public FlixelBar setGradient(@Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color start, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color end)
      Enables a two-color linear gradient for the filled portion along the fill axis. Either argument null disables the gradient and restores solid or textured fill. Rebuilds an internal gradient texture when size or setFillDirection(BarFillDirection) changes.

      Use this if you need something like a health bar, where at the start of the bar the color is green, and at the end of the bar the color is red.

      Parameters:
      start - Color at the start of the gradient axis (left or bottom of the fill direction).
      end - Color at the end of the gradient axis.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setGradient

      public FlixelBar setGradient(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor start, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor end)
      Same as setGradient(Color, Color) using FlixelColor.
      Parameters:
      start - Start color, or null to help disable the gradient.
      end - End color, or null to help disable the gradient.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setBorder

      public FlixelBar setBorder(@Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float thickness)
      Draws a simple axis-aligned frame by tinting four rectangles. Pass null color or non-positive thickness to draw no border (or use clearBorder()).
      Parameters:
      color - Border tint; null clears the border.
      thickness - Width of each border strip in pixels; values below zero are clamped to zero.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • clearBorder

      public FlixelBar clearBorder()
      Removes the border drawn by setBorder(Color, float).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setThresholdColors

      public FlixelBar setThresholdColors(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color fullColor, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color lowColor, float lowPercent)
      Convenience for two-stop threshold coloring: from lowColor at lowPercent up to fullColor at 100% fill. Replaces any previous threshold stops from setThresholdStops(Collection).
      Parameters:
      fullColor - Color used when fill percent is at or above the top stop (full bar).
      lowColor - Color blended in below lowPercent.
      lowPercent - Fill fraction in [0,1] where the low color applies; clamped if out of range.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setThresholdStops

      public FlixelBar setThresholdStops(@NotNull @NotNull Collection<? extends FlixelBar.ThresholdStop> stops)
      Replaces threshold stops from a Collection. Values are copied into an internal Array and sorted by percent. Null entries are skipped.

      For List implementations that also implement RandomAccess, copying uses index loops and avoids iterator allocation on this (typically rare) call. For a libGDX Array, use setThresholdStops(Array).

      Parameters:
      stops - Non-null collection; may be empty to clear thresholds.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setThresholdStops

      public FlixelBar setThresholdStops(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<FlixelBar.ThresholdStop> stops)
      Same as setThresholdStops(Collection) but reads stops from a libGDX Array by index (no iterator on the source).
      Parameters:
      stops - Non-null libGDX array; null entries are skipped.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • clearThresholds

      public FlixelBar clearThresholds()
      Disables threshold-based fill coloring so the bar uses setFilledColor(Color) or gradient only.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setThresholdSmoothing

      public FlixelBar setThresholdSmoothing(float lerp)
      Same as setThresholdSmoothing(float, boolean) with decrease-only smoothing enabled.
      Parameters:
      lerp - Smoothing factor in [0,1] for threshold color transitions.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setThresholdSmoothing

      public FlixelBar setThresholdSmoothing(float lerp, boolean onDecreaseOnly)
      When lerp is below 1, the displayed threshold color eases toward the target color each frame. If onDecreaseOnly is true, smoothing applies when fill percent drops (typical for damage feedback); if false, color also smooths when the percent increases.
      Parameters:
      lerp - Smoothing amount in [0,1]; clamped if out of range.
      onDecreaseOnly - true to smooth mainly on falling health; false to smooth on any change.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setText

      public FlixelBar setText(@Nullable @Nullable Consumer<FlixelString> formatter)
      Sets an optional label rendered on top of the bar. Each update(float), the given callback receives a cleared FlixelString; use FlixelString.concat(java.lang.CharSequence) and FlixelString.set(java.lang.CharSequence) overloads to build the visible text without allocating a String each frame. Text is centered on the bar by default; use setTextOffset(float, float) to nudge it. Pass null to remove overlay text.

      Why a Consumer<FlixelString> and not Supplier<String>? A supplier that returns a new String every frame (common with String.format(java.lang.String, java.lang.Object...) or +) allocates on the heap every frame per bar, which adds up quickly on typical JVMs and on TeaVM. This API reuses one FlixelString per bar, compares the new characters to the last label without building a String when nothing changed, and only then calls FlixelText.setText(CharSequence).

      Parameters:
      formatter - null for no overlay; otherwise invoked each update with the shared scratch buffer.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setTextOffset

      public FlixelBar setTextOffset(float dx, float dy)
      Pixel offset added to the centered text position after setText(java.util.function.Consumer).
      Parameters:
      dx - Horizontal offset in pixels (positive moves right).
      dy - Vertical offset in pixels (positive moves down in Flixel coordinates).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates sprite animation state, then applies max supplier, value tracking, value smoothing, and refreshes overlay text from the setText(java.util.function.Consumer) formatter when set.
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Overrides:
      update in class FlixelSprite
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds since last frame; passed to FlixelSprite.update(float) and smoothing.

    • draw

      public void draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch)
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Override this function to control how the object is drawn. Doing so is rarely necessary but can be very useful.
      Specified by:
      draw in interface FlixelDrawable
      Overrides:
      draw in class FlixelSprite
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch used for rendering.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected. A destroyed FlixelBasic should not be used anymore. Use FlixelBasic.kill() if you only want to disable it temporarily and FlixelBasic.revive() it later.

      Override this function to clean up any resources used by this object, such as textures, fonts, sounds, etc.

      This function is called automatically when FlixelBasic.dispose() or FlixelBasic.reset() is executed, so you don't need to call it manually.

      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelSprite
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()
